### Finding files by what they look like, not what you named them. Launching this tonight.

Hey everyone, I'm launching Dotient on Product Hunt this week and wanted to share what I've been building before the big day. [...] ### AI Agents

Checkmarx Next Generation SAST

What do you think? …

Declan
Dotient

Dotient [...] Dotient is a desktop app that lets you search your files by what they look like, not what you happened to name them two years ago. Everything runs completely offline, nothing leaves your machine. It also has a live graph view of your files, deep PDF search, and canvas workspaces for organizing everything.

I built it solo out of pure frustration with Windows File Explorer and the lack of any tool that actually understands your files without making you do all the work manually.

Would love to hear any questions or feedback before launch. Happy to talk through anything.

results.title: Why Local Businesses Fail at SEO and How to Fix It Fast
results.content: All right. So, you're pouring your heart out into your business. You're protecting your website. You're posting every day. Oh, and you're even investing in ads. Good job. Yet, the phone isn't ringing like it should. It's frustrating. It's exhausting. And honestly, it feels like you're invisible, even in your own community. What if the biggest reason why you're struggling isn't what you're doing, but where your business is actually located? Think your office address is the only thing that affects your local SEO? Think again. There are multiple pieces to this puzzle and some of them might be working against you without you even realizing it. Today, we're laying it all out. Real world examples, some uncomfortable truths, and fixes that don't require a PhD in Google. Let's jump in. Let's [...] Super frustrating. Trust me, I get it. So, what should you do when you get a negative review that is clearly fake? First, you should try flagging it. This only takes 5 minutes. It's definitely worth your time and Google makes it super easy. And if that doesn't work and the review is still there after the appeal, it's time to head over to the Google Business Profile help community. Post a thread asking a product expert for a second opinion about whether or not they think it can be removed. And something to keep in mind, even if you have a really strong case, it is never a guarantee that Google's going to remove the review. The product experts in the Google Business Profile help community have seen tons of cases like this. Yeah. So, for those that don't know, Colin and I actually both are [...] business name can help you rank for St. Louis queries, but keep in mind that you will still struggle to rank within St. Louis itself. It's a good workound, but it is not a perfect fix. All right, switching gears. Fake review time. Because nothing says fun like a onestar review left for your business by someone who never used your services. Probably the most common complaint that I ever hear. This person wasn't our customer. But since Google is completely fine with people staying anonymous, you could literally say you're Beyonce and Google will look at the review and be like, "Hm, seems legit." Exactly. Unless the reviewer flat out admits, "I didn't hire this company. I just felt like complaining." Google's probably not going to help. Super frustrating. Trust me, I get it. So, what should
